Based in Cotonou, Benin, which at the time was referred to as Dahomey, Albarika Records was founded by the French-born, Benin-based producer and entrepreneur, Emmanuel "Manu" Dibango, who had a significant influence on the local music scene. The label was instrumental in releasing records from some of the region's most prominent musicians during the golden age of African music.
Many of the artists signed to Albarika Records were known for blending indigenous African rhythms with Western jazz, funk, and soul influences. This made their sound distinctive and part of a broader movement across West Africa during the 60s and 70s, as music became a form of social and political expression.

"We give a French touch to house"
A two hour ride through the wave of French house music that captivated dancefloors around the world from the mid-90s into the early 2000s. Deeply indebted to Chicago house, French producers brought a louche sense of humour and a penchant for colourful maximalism to the genre. 
The party-forward "filtered house" sound which was poorly imitated ad infinitum into the 2000s was but one aspect of French Touch. St Germain were faithful to the deeper shades of the Chicago source material, and producers like Pépé Bradock used samples to take listeners on intricately arranged and psychedelic dancefloor journeys. 
Aspects of French Touch's history have been purposefully omitted from this two hour mix for the sake of brevity, namely the scene's origins in France's hip hop scene, (Philippe Zdar and DJ Mehdi both produced for legendary French rapper MC Solaar) and French Touch's "second wave" in the mid-2000s, spearheaded by Busy P's Ed Banger Records label, and artists such as Justice.

nts1.n: NTS Guide to: Komuso flute - Shakuhachi
nts1.d: The Komuso were a sect of non-monastic wandering Buddhist monks who emerged in Edo period Japan, who were famous for their face-obscuring woven straw basket masks called tengai. They also differed from monastic Monks, who chose to meditate in silence, as the Komuso played a bamboo flute called a shakuhachi as a form of meditation.

Rising alongside the sophisti-pop strain of British New Wave, sophisti-funk names an early-to-mid-1980s current in R&B and funk that fused post-disco rhythms with high-gloss studio modernism. Defined by digital precision, immaculate surfaces, and a newly cosmopolitan sensibility in Black popular music, it recast funk’s language of grit and excess into one of polish, restraint, and spacious minimalism — total sonic luxury.
Forged in major-label studios with state-of-the-art equipment, with its machine-forged rhythms and melodic extravagance, sophisti-funk laid the sonic and aesthetic foundations for much followed: from street soul and new jack swing through the ’90s boy-band explosion into vaporwave and contemporary digital R&B."

Los Angeles producer & sound artist claire rousay journeys through healing plaintive electronics, quotidian field recordings & minimalist compositions.

nts1.n: Fifth World w/ Ian Kim Judd (R)
nts1.d: A riff on Jon Hassell's Fourth World series. Where Hassell focused on creating ritual music of potential societies that didn't exist; Ian Kim Judd seeks to evoke the notion of potential utopian societies that could exist. In these times of global instability and uncertainty, Fifth World seeks to posit an alternative, with scenes from an optimistic future.

_ “I see the studio must be like a living thing, a life itself… The machine must be live and intelligent. Then I put my mind into the machine and the machine perform reality.”_

Radio Jiro is the two hour show of artist Jiro Bevis, every 4 weeks, Monday 2-4pm. The show features a wide variety of sounds from across the globe with previous specials covering themes such as Meditation, Italian, UK punk, Japanese Techno-Pop, Library and French.

nts1.n: TEARS IN THE CLUB W/ B4mba
nts1.d:
"Tears in the Club" presents a unique blend of structured and chaotic music, combining intense hip hop, drum and bass, footwork, jersey club, and sometimes Afro rhythms. This multi-genre musical show explores diverse musical landscapes, creating a striking contrast between genres, while also showcasing new artists.
From the host: "For this episode, I invited B4mba around a selection of afro-diasporic rap.
Between club and rap, the selection moves through afro-club and afro-influenced rap, alongside gqom, experimental club and percussive bass sounds. B4mba is a Senegalese-Hispanic producer, DJ and vocalist based in Barcelona."

A somber and experimental guest mix from produder and DJ Maral – featuring Iranian classics, early shellac recordings of Iranian classical Music, and field recordings of folk music from different regions of Iran.
Tapping into the liminal feelings of being in the diaspora, mixtures of sadness, anger, fear and nostalgia. A cathartic mix for anyone in the world.
contemporary french + international rap presented by Stephane.

A singular figure in grime who cut his own road through the scene, Dot Rotten had a brief dalliance with mainstream sounds, but was always committed to the underground. A multifaced talent, the artist born Joseph Daniel Ellis-Stevenson, could handle introspective and intricate storytelling, and tackle diss tracks with the fearlessness and sportsmanship that grime demanded. He was also an influential producer, making instrumentals under the name Zeph Ellis. 
NTS selects an hour of tracks from the sadly passed grime MC and producer Dot Rotten.

nts1.n: RUF DUG b2b DANIEL JOHN WILLIS
nts1.d:
Ruf Dug brings the leftfield: old video game soundtracks, dusty analogue dreamers and poolside Balearic sounds. No nonsense from the original tropical cyberpunk.
From the host: "Recorded last night at the Rainy Heart x Loft party in central London. Ruffy was joined on the 1s and 2s by professional man-about-town DANIEL JOHN WILLIS for an all wax evening of high-end sound, Eames chairs and tacos"

Coined by DRB LasGidi in 2014, alté fuses afrobeats, pop, rap, R&B, soul and dancehall into a DIY, genre‑bending sound from Lagos’ creative fringes. The term - slang for “alternative” - celebrates freedom of expression, emerging as a youth‑led reaction to conservative Naija culture. Catch our guide featuring trailblazers Cruel Santino, Odunsi (The Engine), Amaarae, Tems, and more.

Helen Folasade Adu CBE, known professionally as Sade Adu or simply Sade, is an English singer, songwriter, and actress. She is best known as the lead vocalist of the English band Sade. This episode of In Focus looks at her career.

In Iceland, Thomas Stankiewicz contemplates the constant flux of the elements. A composer & musician in his own right, he selectively weaves in between soundtracks, classical music, and evocative ambient sounds, symphonically unveiling untold stories from past and future…
@tommmasi

nts2.n: NTS GUIDE TO: INDIAN CLASSICAL INFLUENCE ON JAZZ (R)
nts2.d: In the mid-1950s musicians such as Yusuf Lateef and John Coltrane started to use instruments, structure and patterns based on traditional Indian music.  Artists sought to expand the boundaries of jazz by incorporating the modal structures, improvisational depth, and spiritual ethos of Indian music. Listen to two hours of Jazz inspired by Indian Classical.

nts2.n: NTS GUIDE TO: SOULFUL SÉGA & ELECTRIC MALOYA FROM THE INDIAN OCEAN (R)
nts2.d:
In the 1970s, artists from Réunion and Mauritius began fusing island styles - Maloya and Séga - with psychedelic rock, funk, and soul. 
Séga, rooted in Afro-Creole rhythms and often sung in Mauritian Creole, developed as a celebratory dance music drawing from European, African, and Indian influences. Maloya, native to Réunion, originated in enslaved Malagasy and African communities - a slower, percussive style traditionally performed with vocals, kayamb and roulér, and associated with ritual, resistance, and later, political activism.
nts2.j: SOULFUL SÉGA & ELECTRIC MALOYA FROM THE INDIAN OCEAN

nts2.n: Saywite w/ Emperatriz Plácido San Martín
nts2.d:
Una hora seleccionada por Camila Palomino…un mapa de encuentros—entre voces y territorios.
From the host: _"Saywite invites Emperatriz Plácido San Martín, a Peruvian trans artist based in Brussels, to create a diaristic mix inspired by house parties in Lima—a meeting between the music of Afro‑Peruvians and migrants from the North, the Andes and the Amazon, tracing the lineages of our friends and families through salsa, cumbia, hip-hop, huayno and more." _
"Emperatriz shares: ¿Qué música sonaba mientras el vapor se escapaba por las ollas en la cocina de tu casa? Se me hace imposible no recordar a quienes extraño con canciones. Cada canción es una invocación a mis muertos y cada vida que yo misma tuve. Sabiduría profunda que cala con cada melodía. Refranes que despiertan nuestra memoria corporal y nos recuerdan que todo es pasajero. Letras que hablan del dolor para saber qué hacer la próxima vez que volvamos a sentirlo. Estos tiempos que miran al futuro olvidan que todo es cíclico. Mientras les comparto invocaciones a tiempos donde el dolor se superó con el deseo, les imagino bailando, escuchando las emociones en cada letra, exorcizando la tristeza de sus cuerpos."

Based in Cotonou, Benin, which at the time was referred to as Dahomey, Albarika Records was founded by the French-born, Benin-based producer and entrepreneur, Emmanuel "Manu" Dibango, who had a significant influence on the local music scene. The label was instrumental in releasing records from some of the region's most prominent musicians during the golden age of African music.
Many of the artists signed to Albarika Records were known for blending indigenous African rhythms with Western jazz, funk, and soul influences. This made their sound distinctive and part of a broader movement across West Africa during the 60s and 70s, as music became a form of social and political expression.

From the host: "Step inside the sonic laboratory of Soviet Ukraine’s Kyivnaukfilm (Eng: Kyiv Science Film, active years 1941-1993) studio — a workshop where composers and sound designers crafted scores for documentary, science, education, and animation films. Ono is a Rīga based program that was originally started by Michael Holland and Paddy Shine in the Manchester studios but has since developed a nomadic exploratory based structure highlighting experimental rhythmic based music made mostly by outsiders.
From the host: "During weeks living off-grid with a colony of Atlantic Grey seals on Wales's Porthdinllaen Peninsula, an extensive archive of seal vocalizations was recorded. A responsive system was then developed allowing a performer to activate the recordings through gestures of pitch, timbre, rhythm and presence"

Tana Yonas collects songs like souvenirs, each recalling precious moments found leafing through dusty foreign record shops or catching sonic fragments weathered by time from the radio of bumpy taxi rides. Listen as she delicately guilds together these memories and shares music from lesser known and under represented musical traditions from across the globe and decades.

Irrevelent, asymmetric selections by Boomkat lifer, Conor Thomas; caretaker of The Death of Rave and R* T*** labels, and resident DJ of The White Hotel & Impiety Hour {AD England} in Manchester, drawing on decades of nocturbulous behaviour and a brend toward short-circuiting genres.
From the host: "Chronicles of Atlantis & Lemuria"
The sound of her brain on standby: A mixture of Industrial textures crackling beneath mutated hip-hop beats, dub, ambient, and more. Not for productive hours.
Long time NTS collaborator and This City Plays producer, Brav brings a monthly showcase of ambient, techno and experimental cuts…
Kaifeng based DJ & producer Yu Su cooks up an hour of warm meanderings once a month.
forwards and backwards through bits of a [mostly] electronic music timeline. reverence for BCR. records i’ve played forever and new records i’ve just found. boil it down. one z in drizle.

A two hour journey through the utterly singular sound of Broadcast. Fronted by the late Trish Keenan, the Birmingham band melded the novel soundscaping of library music and early electronic experiments with their own sensitive take on indie rock and pop. Keenan's untimely passing in 2011 effectively ended the project - but a limited amount of previously unheard material has surfaced in the following years. 
This mix features classics and obscurities from across Broadcast's discography, including exclusive tracks featured in upcoming compilation "Distant Call - Collected Demos 2000-2006".
Contemplative, relaxing and emotional sounds from PC Music producer caro<3…

With releases ranging from D’Angelo to Serge Gainsbourg, Lewis to Betty Davis, Sly Stone to Karen Dalton, LITA prides itself on the breadth of its output.

Austral showcases the wildness and beauty of Latin America’s sounds, and from other places south of somewhere. Aylu probes the depths of music from old times and music from the future.
Avant-garde musician and composer Jim O'Rourke pulls stops by once a month, pulling an hour of picks from his extensive record collection.

The mantra of life unfolds in the symbols of human encounters with nature and the supernatural, reminding us that, in the end, we are but a small part of the vast universe."

nts2.d:

Burroughs and more.

nts2.d: Tim Parker explores the experimental corners of beats and hip hop, weaving together jazz, underground cuts and global sounds - live once a month from London & Amsterdam.

nts2.n: The Big Happy Show w/ FREDFADES
nts2.d: Mr. Big Happy is a graphic designer, artist, DJ, and music archivist from San Francisco, California. While he is an accomplished designer and artist, over the years he has always had a love for music and spends time searching for rare and obscure vinyl. Big Happy’s focus is the late 70s into the late 80s - modern soul, funk, boogie and disco.

One hour of music all across the board, naturally, with Jonny Mons.

nts2.n: NTS Guide to: Golden Age of Malian Music - 1970s
nts2.d: Selections from the "golden age" of Malian music from the late 1960s into the early 1980s. This was the era following Mali's independence in 1960s. President Modibo Keïta, eager to make music a key part of a newly independent national spirit, funded and encouraged the forming of musical communities and orchestras across the country. Many of these groups fused the generations-old griot story telling tradition with modern instrumentation.

Quarterly transmissions from the New York-based composer/producer Alexis Georgopoulos, exploring the unexpected connections between disparate genres & traditions, from Modern Classical to the club-adjacent, experimental to folk, ambient to spiritual jazz and beyond.

8ULENTINA is an interdisciplinary artist, music producer and DJ based in Brooklyn, New York. Known for his dynamic DJ sets and music composition synthesizing a range of genres, geographies and tempos. He co-founded CLUB CHAI (2016-2021), a record label and curatorial platform with Lara Sarkissian and currently runs a SILLAGE, a platform for contemporary sonic artifacts. His NTS show explores the sounds that inspire his production process, focusing on experimentation, percussive intensity, and non linear storytelling through sound.

Cold winds, empty street. Diamondstein, member of the band Melted Bodies, presents Night Shift - ambient techno, sinister industrial sounds, and moody experimental music for night time noir moments.
From the host: "H, aka HEVX, aka ANOTHERREUR is one of my longest running creative collaborators, having done the artwork for every episode of Night Shift. This show is dedicated to what is coming up on ten years of creative partnership and collaboration. Her photography brings Night Shift to life."

Dark Entries reissues archival and contemporary gems that prove to be crucial to our modern idea of bodily music that exists out of time. Electro, italo, cold wave, new beat, house, techno from a one-of-a-kind digger who visits dusty basements to rescue lost tapes.

Death Is Not The End digs up the oldest of the old, serving up your gospel, blues and folk with a crackle. Hear the dust.

Regular transmissions from the Japanese songwriter & composer Eiko Ishibashi, sharing favourites traversing folk, jazz, avant-garde composition and more.

A slow, sensual orbit through forgotten studio experiments and psychedelic grooves.
Floating somewhere between the body, memory, and deep space.
Sit back… and enjoy the journey.
this is Acid Memories !"

A unique dip into the musical history of Ukraine with archival label Shukai, journeying back to the ‘90s, playing music from documentaries, sci-fi films, obscure cartoons and late-night music programming, curated by Dmytro Nikolaienko, Dmytro Prutkin and Alec Tsapenko.
From the host: "A journey through Estonian film music (1967–1986) — from orchestral scores and jazz-infused soundtracks to early electronic experiments. Featuring works by Sven Grünberg, Raimo Kangro, Rein Rannap, and others, written for Estonian, Ukrainian, and Polish films, animation, and television."

universal collage

nts2.n: FIFTH WORLD W/ IAN KIM JUDD
nts2.d:
A riff on Jon Hassell's Fourth World series. Where Hassell focused on creating ritual music of potential societies that didn't exist; Ian Kim Judd seeks to evoke the notion of potential utopian societies that could exist. In these times of global instability and uncertainty, Fifth World seeks to posit an alternative, with scenes from an optimistic future.
From the host: "Thaw tracks with IKJ. First solo mix of the year, marking the ninth (!?) year of the show. Thanks as always for listening."
Jen Monroe writes the album download blog Listen To This!, which focuses on Japanese and new age records: Listen to This

Chime into a world of noise and melancholia. Ranging from ambient to 70s Italian film scores. Perfect time for a long long walk.

LA native Bianca Lexis showcases a monthly, genre-blending hour, exploring the different facets of pop, rock, synth and house music. all in panoramic view, for you.

From their early noise rock and no wave inspired sounds, they progressed into cinematic and lushly orchestrated leftfield pop, with Makino's ethereal vocals as the one constant. nts2.n: NTS GUIDE TO: MODERN ETHIOPIAN ORTHODOX BEGENA (R)
nts2.d:
The begena is a sacred Ethiopian instrument: a ten string lyre, commonly known as the "Harp of David' and central to spiritual music. The deep, devotional tone is most often played unmetered, inspiring its listeners with thoughts of repentance.   
This episode of The NTS Guide To offers a glimpse into a droning, introspective strain of Ethiopian religious music, as you may hear on the streets of Addis Ababa during Lent.
Take a Kokonut Trip with Tim Koh, playing vintage pop, kaleidoscopic rock & folk, and whatever else he feels.
Japanese musician and video game composer June Chikuma, known for her work on the Bomberman series, plays selections from her wide and diverse musical tastes in regular transmissions.

nts2.n: On the Road w/ Adelina S - Gris-gris tour pt. 1
nts2.d:
Traveler and DIY documentarian Adelina S traverses the savannahs and deserts of the Sahel region, recording griots who travel from remote village to village performing to make a living. Through storytelling and field recordings, Adelina shares the winding paths - and the choices - that led to growing close to and be welcomed by this community.
From the host: "Radio show featuring field recordings taken during a road trip with a Fulani Griot band performing for spiritual healers around Fouta-Toro region in Senegal. Through sounds and storytelling the show wil travel to the Sahelian Savanna where centuries old music tradition blends with amplified guitars."

This month’s journey winds upward through the deserts of North Africa, across Sudan and Palestine, and northward still into Lebanon. Along the way, I’ve gathered sounds from both Eastern and Western Europe, with echoes reaching as far as Japan, Jamaica and Brazil. The pace runs a little faster here, the BPM is bit higher in this one to carry me through these final weeks of summer, sweet and fleeting, filled with moonlit gatherings and the first whispers of cooler winds.

nts2.n: Hit Factory W/ DJ Wawa - City Pop Special (R)
nts2.d: From the host: "Post-punk in Peru emerged mainly within the framework of Rock Subterráneo, a movement that took shape in the mid-1980s on the margins of official circuits. Bands inspired by post-punk currents circulating globally forged, from their own social realities, a language that combined introspection, experimentation, and social critique. More than a mood, Peruvian post-punk expressed a collective sensibility shaped by the violence and precariousness that afflicted the country, turning that unrest into a form of creation and affirmation within the local underground."
Los Angeles native & synth pioneer Benedek blends ambient, boogie, funk & more, once a month from our LA studio.

"I could compare my music to white light which contains all colours. Only a prism can divide the colours and make them appear; this prism could be the spirit of the listener." - Arvo Pärt
A figurehead in European minimalism, Arvo Pärt's compositions are slow and meditative, owing to the composer's own mystical experiences with chant music following a conversion to Orthodox Christianity. Now one of the world's most widely performed classical composers, immerse yourself in the output of Estonia's most celebrated musician in a three-hour In Focus special.
An pensive hour of music in translation from Bradfordian's finest export aka UNDERTHEJAGUAR. Spanning across time, geographic, and lexicon borders, this mix takes listeners on a linguistic adventure.
The revered Japanese noise artist stops by for a regular NTS slot, playing some surprising selections from his diverse & deep record collection.

Mikey Young of Total Control and Eddie Current Suppression Ring plays a selection of 60's and 70's soft rock���

From the host: "A two-hour tribute to music pioneer Éliane Radigue, who passed away in Paris on 23 February 2026 at the age of 94. The programme traces her singular path from early tape and feedback pieces to the immersive electronic works of the ARP 2500 years, and nd beyond to the later acoustic cycles."

nts2.n: THE OPERA SHOW w/ Daniel Oduntan, Seyi Adelekun & Raheel Khan
nts2.d:
Hannah Catherine Jones splits things open for the Opera Show: an exploration of the best in opera old and new, as well as an unearthing of the operatic in other records from soul, jazz or further afield.
From the host: "This month I'm excited to welcome guest hosts Daniel Oduntan, Seyi Adelekun & Raheel Khan - all amazing interdisciplinary artist-musicians who are currently preparing for live events as part of Somerset House's experimental sound and music series Assembly on March 26-28th (see links for more info)."

“I start associating your voice with ….”, for a while ML has been part of the NTS community, jingling around, airing his monthly 150 Session out of Berlin’s North, where all seasons give reasons. For clarification, creation, inspiration. One hour of poetic and musical explorations, questions, and curiosities. Circling around daring electronics, ethno-sonics, jazz-constructivism, minimalism, open structured digital dopamine, risky dub excitement and other sounds, that still need to be written. No country, no flag – outernational without a cause!
From the host: "Today 150 Session drifts into a quieter space: Ambient currents. Slow drifts. Sounds for contemplation. Music to open a small pocket of calm for our troubled times. Let the frequencies soften the noise."

Their highly-curated selection ranges from porros, gaitas, rebajadas, to rare tracks and beloved classics - all on 45s.
Ritmo Santanero stands out as an artistic contender in the Southern California sonidero scene as the trio expands out of Santa Ana and into Long Beach, and Los Angeles - bringing the best all-vinyl cumbia night to a town near you. Spinning a mix of cumbia genres from of all corners of the world, Ritmo Santanero is reviving the once-dormant sonidero culture of the 90’s, yet deviating from the announcement-heavy tradition by letting the music do the talking.

nts2.n: Funk Freaks Radio w/ DJ Zee (R)

nts2.d:
On today's Radio Discostan we present an hour of music from Bangkok's Arab-Malay scene assembled by Peter Doolan of Monrakplengthai. In these songs, we hear Thai pop/rock sounds layered among rhythms, melodies and textures from throughout the Muslim world. Influences from Malaysia, Indonesia, Egypt, Lebanon, and India are all discernable in the mix.
Thai Arab-Malay acts had their heyday in the '70s and '80s entertaining cosmopolitan visitors from the ummah on "Soi Arab" (Sukhumvit Lane 3/1), and popular nightlife bands of the era have remained a staple at mosque fairs and other cultural events around the central provinces ever since.
The selections in this set are primarily digitized from cassettes that were for sale at Phrakhanong's Yanavy Tape & Video or from various second-hand vendors around the capital.Peter Doolan covers the world of Thai music on cassette at monrakplengthai.blogspot.com.

The title of the compilation became shorthand for the sound, which would become the blueprint for contemporary "indie" as a genre: guitar-based music characterised by jangly tones and power pop song structures, taking healthy inspiration from sunny 1960s pop music.
nts2.j: Primitive Painters - Jangle Pop & Indie Rock from the UK Post Punk Era

Based in Cotonou, Benin, which at the time was referred to as Dahomey, Albarika Records was founded by the French-born, Benin-based producer and entrepreneur, Emmanuel "Manu" Dibango, who had a significant influence on the local music scene. The label was instrumental in releasing records from some of the region's most prominent musicians during the golden age of African music.
Many of the artists signed to Albarika Records were known for blending indigenous African rhythms with Western jazz, funk, and soul influences. This made their sound distinctive and part of a broader movement across West Africa during the 60s and 70s, as music became a form of social and political expression.

A unique dip into the musical history of Ukraine with archival label Shukai, journeying back to the ‘90s, playing music from documentaries, sci-fi films, obscure cartoons and late-night music programming, curated by Dmytro Nikolaienko, Dmytro Prutkin and Alec Tsapenko.
From the host: "Step inside the sonic laboratory of Soviet Ukraine’s Kyivnaukfilm (Eng: Kyiv Science Film, active years 1941-1993) studio — a workshop where composers and sound designers crafted scores for documentary, science, education, and animation films. From eerie synth textures to inventive tape collages, these rare recordings (1967–1990) reveal a secret world of avant-garde music created under censorship yet brimming with experiments."

Juana Molina is one of South America's most remarkable and exploratory popular musicians. For over three decades, the Argentine-born artist has approached songwriting and music production with a childlike curiosity and sage open-mindednes, garnering a beloved following in Argentina and beyond. 
Her music, which often compared to Björk, is a patchwork of folk, ambient, electronic and indie rock, and avant-garde in a way that is truly her own. Listening to Molina's music, which is often suffused with humour and oddity, it perhaps comes as no surprise that she first rose to prominence as a comedian, featuring on Artgentinian sketch comedy shows in the early 1990s.

nts2.d:
Lock in for a monthly transmission of leftfield sonics from Josh Da Costa live from LA.
From the host: "Mostly a post-tour recap of a recent trip through UK & Europe ! A few of my recent weird finds."

Tana Yonas collects songs like souvenirs, each recalling precious moments found leafing through dusty foreign record shops or catching sonic fragments weathered by time from the radio of bumpy taxi rides. Listen as she delicately guilds together these memories and shares music from lesser known and under represented musical traditions from across the globe and decades.

nts2.n: Improvisational Aesthetics w/ LAGOS (Leo123 x RADIUS)
nts2.d: Chicago DJ, producer and performers RADIUS and Leo 123 present their take on jazz, taking in the breadth of the genre, from free jazz to modal to Latin styles and more.
Folk, indie rock, jazz and beyond selected by Kansas City songwriter Kevin Morby in a special guest show.

nts2.n: Sacred Space: Spring Special w/ Radha
nts2.d:
Enter Radha's spiritual soundscape - of special tunes near and dear to her.
From the host: "Back this month with a sunny spring sacred space special. slow and bright

Phil Cho & Russell Alexander share monthly recordings from their hilltop soundsystem in Echo Park, California.
From the host: "Full two hour guest mix from Spiritual World."

PPU signed vocalist & producer dreamcastmoe transmits a monthly program directly from LA via DC..
From the host: "Hola, some people call me Drink Water, I’m based in the DMV, and I performed this mix with a X:ONE 92 and some slightly busted CDJ 900s (not NXS, the originals).
I honestly have no idea how I came to this conclusion of a mix, but if anything, I think it’s a fair representation of the sound and energy in DC that made me fall in love with the experience of dance music; long live SEQUENCE and Sprüdel."

nts2.n: ROOMIES W/ MOBILEGIRL - MUSIC FOR THE STUDIO (R)
nts2.d: mobilegirl invites you into her home with soundtracks for various rooms.
Tokyo-based DJ & composer Abiu taking you to a higher state, playing tracks from across the house axis.

subtle.d: In:flux Audio are a record label based in Leeds, UK founded by production duo Tik&Borrow. Since the label's inception in October 2013, they have dabbled in multiple dance music styles with the emphasis always being on the tracks being unique, and packed full of bass. Today the primary output for the label is from their Dubstep roster and this is focus of their show on Subtle - giving the artists and remixers chance to showcase their sounds with an hour guest mix, all hosted by label bossman Tik.

I’m a 19 year old DJ and producer from Newcastle upon Tyne, mainly playing and producing music around the 140bpm mark. I started out playing old 2-step and jungle records, before moving my focus to mainly dubstep and grime. Along with my friends Pirata and White Label, I run Newcastle’s only current 140 night, Well Irie.

subtle.n: THE CULTURE HEAD w. SAINT VOX
Skelecta presents: South London Dance Factory

subtle.d: With a monthly show on Subtle, Niceup is a London based DJ with a unique style. Pushing UKG, Breakbeat, Bass, Electro and 140. Weaving through genres showcasing new releases and older cuts.

Jedah started DJ'ing at the tender age of 18 years of age specialising in UK Garage/Grime. 

Jedah is currently the DJ for Grime Original Hollowman Jendor of the former Grime Collective Essentials/ OG's.

Responsible for “that massive grime tune with the Psycho strings”, UK producers/DJs Trends & Boylan are well versed in what the scene has to offer. They use this platform to showcase music from across the 140 spectrum from Grime to Dubstep. Anything goes!!

XOSÉ is a DJ and aspiring producer mixing and making mostly UK Garage, Grime and UK Funky. FORWARD PRESSURE largely focuses on the aforementioned UK born genres, though you can expect a variety of UK influenced sounds.

Sempra is a producer & DJ based in London, specialising in the fusion of UK Garage, Jungle and Breakbeat with dreamy vocal melodies and the beautiful instrumentation found in Soul and contemporary RnB. Sempra’s consistent output has been released by labels such as Future Classic and Headroom, gaining industry support from the likes of Nia Archives, Sherelle, Yung Singh and Dr Dubplate.

Cicely is a northern raised London based music photographer and DJ. From learning to mix during lockdown, she has since played at clubs across the country in venues such as Fabric, Corsica and Fold.

Cicely runs a collective and events night under the name NRG CRU which focus heavily on the high energy rave sound. She is highly influenced by the northern sound she grew up with, and you can hear this within her sets. Expect heavy blends of techno and hardcore while she shines the light on her favourite northern artists.

Live & Direct is the show that promises to make you dance and make you laugh, with your fave tunes that make you go 'Rahhh ain't hear this in time' and all the cheesy jokes from me, Hannah :) Lock in for the sickest guests and the most relatable stories about daily slips and hilarious situations I find myself in.

Bristol-born and London-based, Not Bad For A Girl resident DaiSu has been involved in the music scene since 2018 and has featured on stations such as Radio 1 Dance, BBC 6 Music and Melodic Distraction, as well as playing on line ups for Keep Hush, Skiddle and Red Bull. Her penchant for bass, breaks and 2-step has landed her gigs supporting acts such as Interplanetary Criminal, Main Phase, Craigie Knowes and Big Ang. In September 2022, her debut EP ‘Lightwork’ was released with Gather Records and was featured in DJ Mag's staff picks that same month.

Vinyl DJ from Leigh-on-Sea. Loves collecting records with a wide array of taste ranging from House and Techno to Grime, Dubstep and Jungle. Garage holds a special place in his heart and makes up the largest part of my collection and DJ sets.

DJ & Producer hailing from Watford influenced by Hip-Hop, Dubstep, Grime with releases on NSX records & Ghost Phone, spinning electronic genres from R&B edits, Drum and Bass and everything in between.
Highlander brings you 2 hours of the freshest Jungle, Hardcore, Dubstep and Breaks. 3rd Saturday 7-9pm lock in!

2Step Selects is a monthly show hosted by the Choki Biki Records crew. Founded in Dublin, Ireland, CBR consists of DJ Sharpson, 2step.png, Frame Shift, and MICK. The show consists of an all round breaks, electro, ukg and jungle hour, followed by an Irish showcase, demonstrating all the best Ireland has to offer collective/party/Dj & Producer wise!

Saraphim (aka RNS & RDubz) are two Essex based Producer/DJ’s currently focused on Dubstep & the 140 spectrum, mixing ethnical and grime influences into their music. You can expect anything from deep and melodic meditative vibes to high energy driven music.
London based DJ who enjoys exploring the darker, bassier corners of Jungle, Breaks, Techno and Drum & Bass, especially when it is laced with a bit of acid.

subtle.n: The Ice Box Sessions with Frigid

subtle.d: Hailing from northern Italy, STANDARD is a collective of artists, DJs and producers that aims at the promotion of bass music: Garage, UK Funky, Breaks, Leftfield Bass, Leftfield Techno, Dubstep, everything in between and beyond. They focus on the Italian Bass scene, showcasing new up-and-coming artists as well as established talents from all over the peninsula.

Oddkut is a producer & DJ hailing from Balakovo City, Russia. He's part of the Russian collective "United Gang" who are a Moscow-based community of local musicians, DJ's and promoters teamed up by their genuine love & interest in UK underground music, "TAЭT VREMYA" art group and the creator of the Moscow parties "Judder".
"The calm before summer"

Kishan is a DJ and Producer currently based in London. Actively involved in the music scene already, he produces and mixes a wide range of genres including UK Garage, Drum & Bass, House, Disco and Afrobeat.

subtle.n: Out of Phase With Sam Ling

subtle.d: Sessions w/ Amy B is an underground multi-genre music show, showcasing the most prolific guests from all corners of the music industry.

Originally linking up in Wellington and playing shows with the Breaking Beats crew, Swampman and Zero Crumbs have reunited – bringing their smooth mixing and flexible selections to East London. 

In their monthly show, the pair explore the deep, dark, and soulful ends of DNB. Expect fresh underground tunes, deep cut classics, and a bit of banter in Kiwi accents.

subtle.n: Dun Roamin w/ Tomi From Brockley

subtle.d:
Midlands born, London based producer and DJ influenced by UK funky, broken techno, garage and early dubstep. 

Has released on Keysound recordings, RKS, eatmybeat, ec2a, Durkle Disco and Dimsum records

Established in 2013, eatmybeat is a hybrid record label & club brand that was brought together by a group of friends with a passion for electronic music and building community. With diverse tastes, they support and champion the underground bass music scene through events and releases on the label. Refusing to be pigeonholed into a specific sound, they showcase everything from hip-hop, bass & breaks, through to techno, jungle, dubstep and grime. As long as it's tasty and full fat, you best believe eatmybeat are snacking on it.

subtle.n: BASICS RECORDS: L MAJOR, TAMEN & SIDESHOW

subtle.d: Born in Sunderland, based in London, Kitschko's productions and selections display a deep appreciation of UK club culture, and an affinity for late night, introspective sounds.

As a drummer since childhood, Chacho has always had an insatiable appetite for new and unique beats.
Combining his love of global percussion, dark grooves and deeper low end frequencies, 
his sets feature a blend of dubstep, grime, garage, breaks, hip hop and other flavours from around the world.
Hailing from Belfast but making moves in Nottingham, Nikki O is a DJ and radio presenter whose sets blend 4/4 rhythms, breakbeats, and the bassier side of techno.

DJ & Producer, B-Say has been a Subtle resident DJ for over 3 years.

His radio shows tend to focus on the 140 bass music scene and aims to showcase unreleased/forthcoming music from upcoming artists, as well as premiering his own unreleased material. 

More recently B-Say has ventured out from solely DJ'ing Dubstep, with his bi-monthly 'Dub & Steppers Show' which he uses to premier his own productions.

Slick is a London-based Producer and DJ combining the sounds of Grime and Drill.
Expect sets to span from sparse wave and weightless to classic NY and UK Drill, with everything in between
J:KENZO is a producer and record label owner (Artikal Music & Lion Charge) from the UK. Known for his minimalistic dark tribal sound in the both the dubstep and drum and bass scenes since 2006.

subtle.n: Pattern Recognition w/ R A L L Y
Mid Morning Matters w/ Alfie Danger
Taking the Peel w/ Badly Drawn Banana

subtle.d:
DJ and radio producer TILDA will be live from the studio to kick off your weekend. She’ll cover all corners of electronic music as well as showcasing unmissable new tracks & chatting about motives for the weekend. 

Each month TILDA will be closing the show with a signature mix.

Live & Direct is the show that promises to make you dance and make you laugh, with your fave tunes that make you go 'Rahhh ain't hear this in time' and all the cheesy jokes from me, Hannah :) Lock in for the sickest guests and the most relatable stories about daily slips and hilarious situations I find myself in.

Following his widely acclaimed breakout moment winning the 2013 DMC World Online Championships, Jon1st quickly built an international reputation as a ‘DJ’s DJ’. Not only proving himself as one of the world’s most diverse and musical turntablists, Jon also established himself as an on the pulse club DJ mixing across the alternative bass music spectrum and as an in demand live performer around the world, as well as recording acclaimed guest mixes for everyone from DJ Mag to Boiler Room to Ninja Tune’s Solid Steel to Mary Anne Hobbs to Keep Hush.

subtle.n: 5 Decades of Dub w/ Joe Vincent

subtle.d: ixxi and Fei take you on a percussive journey with their show syncop8, moving from downtempo ambient to breaks, polyrhythms, and high energy beats, exploring every rhythmic and textural nuance in between.
Representing UK Bass Music Culture across a range of styles, sounds and tempos.

Rolling through varied tempo's and genres, Jimmy 2 Shoes brings dance floor orientated vibes and flows to wriggle your toes to. Expect eclectic selections and the freshest sounds from the UK underground.

subtle.n: The Wheel Up Show w/ Tripta
subtle.d: Tripta is a Toronto based producer/DJ focusing on the darker sounds of Grime. Also blending elements of Drill, Dubstep and even Jungle all together for a unique sound you won't hear anywhere else. Signed to Slimzee's 'Slimzo's Recordings' and ‘Rotpot Records’.

Sound Arkitek is a Chicago hip-hop DJ with a love for all things drum & bass. 

With over 20 years experience playing house parties, raves, clubs and bars he has an encyclopedic knowledge of music but has always been a participant in jungle culture. In the early 2000s he was exposed to the genre primarily through Dieselboy and Danny The Wildchild mixes as well as the “Jungle Book” series of mixtapes. He became obsessed after hearing Bad Company’s “The Nine” and “Four Days,” as well as Pendulum’s “Vault.” 

As time went on, he got deeper into the scene, going to raves while buying records and posting on Drum & Bass Arena and DNBForum before turning 21 at which point he began going to Lava Lounge for Bass By The Pound’s “Seminar” series. The first legal drum & bass party he attended was Chase & Status at the aforementioned Lava Lounge, and he will never forget the energy when the duo played DJ Hazard’s “Killers Don’t Die” and it got a rewind. His love for the scene led to him playing drum & bass parties, opening for artists like Original Sin, Gridlok, and LTJ Bukem among others. 

His Subtle Radio show is an hour of the finest tunes in the scene at the moment. 

To keep up, follow @djsoundarkitek on Instagram!

Dubversified showcases Canadian West Coast DJ talent specializing in Bass Music. Hosted by Mirage, founder of Dubversified Culture, a community-led organization focusing on music, art, and events. Mirage, originally from Québec, blends house and hip-hop since 2010, now exploring 140bpm and Deep D&B.

subtle.n: Into The Lion's Den w/ Restrikt

subtle.d: Potent Funk records’ Matt Frost - Well known for his technical abilities and Sleazy bass music style, Frost covers all angles from UK alt Rap to the LA beat scene.

subtle.n: BASICS RECORDS: L MAJOR, TAMEN & SIDESHOW

subtle.d: Get ready to embark on a captivating sonic journey of underground music with Charlie Kelly. Including new and timeless sounds from the depths of rap, drill, grime, and experimental genres from the vibrant streets of the UK and beyond. Charlie unearths the very best in music that pushes boundaries. Off the mic, Charlie is a Radio Producer and Social Media Executive with an eclectic music taste spanning across many different cultures, decades, and styles. Hailing from North London with a love for audio and pop culture, you can expect to hear tune after tune when you’re locked into Charlie Kelly on Subtle.
BirdE brings an hour of the freshest Drum and Bass covering everything from liquid to techy steppers - the deeper the better.

Originally from Cambridge, Counter Culture's selection deftly navigates across a myriad of influences and exclusives from himself & friends.
Expect some of the freshest rolling soul straight to your headspace.

subtle.n: Strictly Business Show w/ Qua Rush & Flipz MC
What's The Weather, Selecta? w/ Megan Wroe

subtle.d:
Hailing from Mumbai, now living in London, Chaitu is energised by the UK sound and scene.

With a love of all things weird, weighty and groovy; his mixes and productions bridge electro, techno, breaks, UKG and Jungle, with sprinklings of everything in between.

Chaitu champions provocative beats that break down the barriers of style, genre and expectation, all while keeping you moving.
